steps.title: Recognize a difference of squares
steps.content: Write each term as a perfect square: $256w^2 = (16w)^2$ and $676 = 26^2$. So $256w^2 - 676 = (16w)^2 - 26^2$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
Expand each candidate and compare to $256w^2 - 676$.
  • Choice A — $(16w-26)(16w-26)$: $(16w-26)^2 = 256w^2 - 832w + 676$. Has a $w$ term and wrong constant sign. Eliminate.
  • Choice B — $(8w-13)(8w+13)$: $64w^2 - 169$. Wrong leading coefficient and constant. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $(8w-13)(8w-13)$: $64w^2 - 208w + 169$. Wrong on all three terms. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $(16w-26)(16w+26)$: $256w^2 - 676$. Exact match.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
